findings.summary: Desktop Electron app has moderate decoupling quality. IPC handlers use DI pattern (strength) but 50+ magic-string channels (weakness). Test coverage is minimal (3 files, 0% on IPC/windows/menu/updater). Hardcoded assumptions in GitHub OAuth client ID, app identity, macOS paths, protocol names.
Preload bridge surface is fully typed (ElectronAPI interface) but has 38 methods with 50+ magic-string channels. No shared channel constants file. Renderer accesses all via window.api.
Test coverage is minimal: only 3 test files covering pure validation functions and shell-utils. Zero tests for IPC handlers, window management, menu, updater, server/sidecar, or the preload bridge.
GitHub OAuth client ID (Iv23li7XUy0RKw5TlZ3K) is hardcoded plaintext. App IDs/names duplicated across index.ts and migrate.ts. macOS paths (/.app) hardcoded in apps.ts.
Git history shows evolution from Tauri to Electron with recent modularization (github-ipc.ts, ipc-validation.ts extraction). Effect-TS used in main process but not consistently.
